output.formatted:
Alessandro Del Piero played for Italy's national team.

SPARQL query over wikidata:
```sparql
PREFIX wdt: <http://www.wikidata.org/prop/direct/>
PREFIX wd: <http://www.wikidata.org/entity/>
SELECT ?country WHERE {
  wd:Q624 wdt:P1532 ?country
}
```

messages.content:
You are a question answering assistant. Your job is to generate a SPARQL query to answer a given user question.

You should follow a step-by-step approach to generate the SPARQL query:
1. Determine possible entities and properties implied by the user question.
2. Search for the entities and properties in the knowledge graphs. Where applicable, constrain the searches with already identified entities and properties.
3. Gradually build up the SPARQL query using the identified entities and properties. Start with simple queries and add more complexity as needed. Execute intermediate queries to get feedback and to verify your assumptions. You may need to refine or rethink your current plan based on the query results and go back to step 2 if needed, possibly multiple times.
4. Use the answer or cancel function to finalize your answer and stop the generation process.

Available knowledge graphs:
- "wikidata" at https://qlever.dev/api/wikidata
  - "entity" index (type="fuzzy"): Entities indexed by their labels and synonyms
  - "property" index (type="embedding", modalities="text"): Properties indexed by their labels, synonyms, and identifiers

Index types used:
- "embedding": Retrieves items by cosine similarity between their embeddings and the query embedding. The embedding model used depends on the index and may support text, images, or both.
- "fuzzy": Retrieves items by overlap between their label words and the query keywords. The query keywords must not match label words exactly, but some fuzziness is allowed. The longer a query keyword is, the more it can deviate from a label word and still be considered a match, though it will also contribute less to the overall score. No special query operators like AND/OR are supported.

SPARQL prefixes for use in function calls:
- bd: http://www.bigdata.com/rdf#
- cc: http://creativecommons.org/ns#
- data: https://www.wikidata.org/wiki/Special:EntityData/
- dc: http://purl.org/dc/elements/1.1/
- dct: http://purl.org/dc/terms/
- foaf: http://xmlns.com/foaf/0.1/
- geo: http://www.opengis.net/ont/geosparql#
- geof: http://www.opengis.net/def/function/geosparql/
- gn: http://www.geonames.org/ontology#
- hint: http://www.bigdata.com/queryHints#
- imdb: https://www.imdb.com/
- math: http://www.w3.org/2005/xpath-functions/math#
- ontolex: http://www.w3.org/ns/lemon/ontolex#
- owl: http://www.w3.org/2002/07/owl#
- p: http://www.wikidata.org/prop/
- pq: http://www.wikidata.org/prop/qualifier/
- pqn: http://www.wikidata.org/prop/qualifier/value-normalized/
- pqv: http://www.wikidata.org/prop/qualifier/value/
- pr: http://www.wikidata.org/prop/reference/
- prn: http://www.wikidata.org/prop/reference/value-normalized/
- prov: http://www.w3.org/ns/prov#
- prv: http://www.wikidata.org/prop/reference/value/
- ps: http://www.wikidata.org/prop/statement/
- psn: http://www.wikidata.org/prop/statement/value-normalized/
- psv: http://www.wikidata.org/prop/statement/value/
- qb: http://purl.org/linked-data/cube#
- qfn: http://qlever.cs.uni-freiburg.de/function#
- ql: http://qlever.cs.uni-freiburg.de/builtin-functions/
- qlss: https://qlever.cs.uni-freiburg.de/spatialSearch/
- rdf: http://www.w3.org/1999/02/22-rdf-syntax-ns#
- rdfs: http://www.w3.org/2000/01/rdf-schema#
- ref: http://www.wikidata.org/reference/
- s: http://www.wikidata.org/entity/statement/
- schema: http://schema.org/
- skos: http://www.w3.org/2004/02/skos/core#
- v: http://www.wikidata.org/value/
- void: http://rdfs.org/ns/void#
- wd: http://www.wikidata.org/entity/
- wdno: http://www.wikidata.org/prop/novalue/
- wdt: http://www.wikidata.org/prop/direct/
- wdtn: http://www.wikidata.org/prop/direct-normalized/
- wikibase: http://wikiba.se/ontology#
- xsd: http://www.w3.org/2001/XMLSchema#

Additional rules to follow:
- Explain your thought process before and after each step and function call.
- Do not ask the user for clarification until you have made an initial attempt at completing the task. When the task input is incomplete or ambiguous, proceed based on reasonable assumptions.
- Do not use "SERVICE wikibase:label { bd:serviceParam wikibase:language ..." in SPARQL queries. It is unsupported by the used QLever SPARQL endpoints. Use rdfs:label or similar properties to get labels instead.
- Always execute your final SPARQL query before giving an answer to make sure it returns the expected results.
- The SPARQL query should always return the actual identifiers / IRIs of the items in its result. It additionally may return labels or other human-readable information, but they are optional and should be put within optional clauses unless explicitly requested by the user.
- Do not stop early if there are still obvious improvements to be made to the SPARQL query. For example, keep refining your SPARQL query if its result contains irrelevant items or is missing items you expected.
- Do not perform additional computation (e.g. filtering, sorting, calculations) on the result of the SPARQL query to determine the answer. All computation should be done solely within SPARQL.
- For questions with a "True" or "False" answer the SPARQL query should be an ASK query.

content.message:
I found Alessandro Del Piero in Wikidata with the identifier wd:Q624. Now I need to find which property links a person to their national team. 

Let me search for properties related to "national team" or "country" that might be associated with a player:

tool_calls.name: search_property
tool_calls.result:
Top 10 property alternatives:
1. national team appearances (wd:P1129 as wdt/p/ps/pq/pr/wdtn/psn/pqn/prn/psv/pqv/prv, matched via "national team caps"), also known as "national team caps":
  - total number of games officially played by a sportsman for national team
2. country for sport (wd:P1532 as wdt/p/ps/pq/pr/wdtn/psn/pqn/prn/psv/pqv/prv, matched via "sport nationality"), also known as "representative nationality", "sport country", "sporting nationality", "sport nationality", "sports country", etc.:
  - country a person or a team represents when playing a sport
3. member of sports team (wd:P54 as wdt/p/ps/pq/pr/wdtn/psn/pqn/prn/psv/pqv/prv, matched via "sport team"), also known as "club played for", "member of team", "of team", "part of team", "played for", etc.:
  - sports teams or clubs that the subject represents or represented
4. participating team (wd:P1923 as wdt/p/ps/pq/pr/wdtn/psn/pqn/prn/psv/pqv/prv, matched via "teams"), also known as "teams":
  - like 'Participant' (P710) but for teams. For an event like a ... of sports team' (P54) as a qualifier for the individuals)
5. National-Football-Teams.com team ID (wd:P12308 as wdt/p/ps/pq/pr/wdtn/psn/pqn/prn/psv/pqv/prv, matched via "National-Football-Teams.com national team ID"), also known as "National-Football-Teams.com national team ID", "N-F-T team ID", "NFT team ID":
  - identifier for a association football (soccer) national team in the National-Football-Teams.com database
6. represents (wd:P1268 as wdt/p/ps/pq/pr/wdtn/psn/pqn/prn/psv/pqv/prv, matched via "team of"), also known as "means", "refers to", "representing", "represents organisation", "stands for", etc.:
  - organization, individual, or concept that an entity represents
7. country of citizenship (wd:P27 as wdt/p/ps/pq/pr/wdtn/psn/pqn/prn/psv/pqv/prv, matched via "national of"), also known as "citizen of", "citizenship", "country", "(legal) nationality", "nation", etc.:
  - the object is a country that recognizes the subject as its citizen
8. anthem (wd:P85 as wdt/p/ps/pq/pr/wdtn/psn/pqn/prn/psv/pqv/prv, matched via "team anthem"), also known as "club anthem", "march", "national anthem", "official hymn", "official song", etc.:
  - subject's official anthem
9. occupant (wd:P466 as wdt/p/ps/pq/pr/wdtn/psn/pqn/prn/psv/pqv/prv, matched via "home team"), also known as "home team", "houses", "inhabitant", "lessee", "location of", etc.:
  - person or organization occupying property
10. partner in business or sport (wd:P1327 as wdt/p/ps/pq/pr/wdtn/psn/pqn/prn/psv/pqv/prv, matched via "team-mate"), also known as "business partner", "collaborator", "colleague", "partnered with", "partner in business", etc.:
  - professional collaborator

content.message:
I need to find the property that links a person to their national team. From the search results, "country for sport" (P1532) seems to be the most relevant property as it specifically relates to the country a person represents in sports.

Now, let me search for the properties of Alessandro Del Piero (wd:Q624) that are related to his national team or country:

tool_calls.name: search_property_of_entity
tool_calls.result:
Top 10 property alternatives:
1. country for sport (wd:P1532 as wdt/p, matched via "sport nationality"), also known as "representative nationality", "sport country", "sporting nationality", "sport nationality", "sports country", etc.:
  - country a person or a team represents when playing a sport
2. member of sports team (wd:P54 as wdt/p, matched via "sport team"), also known as "club played for", "member of team", "of team", "part of team", "played for", etc.:
  - sports teams or clubs that the subject represents or represented
3. country of citizenship (wd:P27 as wdt/p, matched via "national of"), also known as "citizen of", "citizenship", "country", "(legal) nationality", "nation", etc.:
  - the object is a country that recognizes the subject as its citizen
4. National-Football-Teams.com player ID (wd:P2574 as wdt/p, matched via "National Football Teams ID"), also known as "National-Football-Teams.com ID", "National Football Teams ID", "NFT ID", "NFT player ID":
  - identifier for a football player, at National-Football-Teams.com
5. sport (wd:P641 as wdt/p), also known as "play", "plays", "sport played", "sports":
  - sport that the subject participates or participated in or is associated with
6. WorldFootball.net person ID (old) (wd:P2020 as wdt/p, matched via "worldfootball.net/"), also known as "https://www.worldfootball.net/", "https://www.worldfootball.net/player_summary/", "worldfootball.net/", "WorldFootball.net ID", "www.worldfootball.net/":
  - former identifier for an association football (soccer) player ... or Livefutbol.com (redirects to new URL and ID)
7. position played on team / speciality (wd:P413 as wdt/p), also known as "fielding position", "player position", "position", "position (on team)", "specialism", etc.:
  - position or specialism of a player on a team
8. participant in (wd:P1344 as wdt/p, matched via "competed in"), also known as "attended", "competed in", "competes in", "participant of", "participant of event", etc.:
  - event in which a person, organization or creative work was/is a participant; inverse of P710 or P1923
9. L'Équipe football player ID (wd:P3665 as wdt/p, matched via "www.lequipe.fr/"), also known as "https://www.lequipe.fr/", "https://www.lequipe.fr/Football/", "lequipe.fr football player ID", "www.lequipe.fr/":
  - ID of a football player by L'Équipe
10. FootballDatabase.eu person ID (wd:P3537 as wdt/p, matched via "footballdatabase.eu/"), also known as "footballdatabase.eu/", "FootballDatabase.eu ID", "https://www.footballdatabase.eu/", "https://www.footballdatabase.eu/en/", "https://www.footballdatabase.eu/en/player/", etc.:
